message:
Add MiniMax M3 VL: draft scaffold with modular, tiny fixtures, MXFP8 dequant

- Modular implementation reusing minimax_m2 for the M2-shaped pieces (router,
  decoder layer skeleton, expert layout) and adding M3 deltas: shared experts
  + sigmoid routing scaling, dense-MLP-for-first-N-layers, swigluoai with
  alpha/limit clamp, partial RoPE (rotary_dim < head_dim), per-head Gemma-style
  QK norm, and the sparse-attention lightning-index branch (full dense math
  fallback for v1; top-k block selection deferred).
- CLIP-like vision tower with Conv3d patch embed and a 3D RoPE over (T, H, W)
  patch grids, plus LLaVA-style projector + spatial patch merger.
- Fast image / video processors and a multimodal processor ported from the
  snapshot's preprocessing code; auto registrations for config / model /
  processor / image / video.
- Tiny model fixtures: build_tiny_model.py (transformers layout) and
  build_tiny_model_sglang.py (re-keyed for sglang FusedMoE), so parity
  checks across backends do not require the 100B-param real model.
- dequantize_mxfp8.py converts the MXFP8 ([1, 32]-blocked E4M3) checkpoint
  shipped by MiniMax to bf16 -- transformers has MXFP4 today but no MXFP8,
  so the first port dequantizes up front.

Status: text + vision forward passes work end-to-end on the tiny model;
both AutoModelForImageTextToText and AutoModelForCausalLM are wired.
Sparse attention is dense-math only and MTP modules are skipped.
author: Arthur Zucker

message:
Strip dead branches + port lightning indexer from deepseek_v4

* Drop config fields that the real M3 VL checkpoint has only a single
  value for and inline the constants:
    - text: use_gemma_norm, use_qk_norm, qk_norm_type, attention_output_gate,
      hidden_act, scoring_func, partial_rotary_factor
    - composite: multimodal_projector_bias, projector_hidden_act,
      vision_feature_layer, vision_feature_select_strategy,
      process_image_mode, img_token_compression_config, image_grid_pinpoints,
      image_seq_length
    - vision: rope_mode, vision_segment_max_frames, hidden_act
* Norms: single Gemma-style RMSNorm class (drop the `gemma:` switch and the
  plain RMSNorm alias). Activation in the dense MLP / experts / projector is
  always swigluoai / GELU; remove the alternate branches.
* Attention: per-head Gemma QK norm and partial RoPE are always on; remove
  the per_layer / no-norm / full-RoPE branches.
* Replace the dense fallback of the sparse-attention layer with a proper
  lightning indexer modeled on `DeepseekV4Indexer`:
    - MiniMaxM3VLIndexer scores key blocks via idx_q · idx_k, max-reduces
      over `sparse_block_size`, takes top-k blocks per query, then forces
      the first `sparse_init_block` and last `sparse_local_block` blocks
      visible.
    - Encodes the selection as an additive `[B, 1, S, S]` block_bias
      (`-inf` outside the allowed blocks), summed onto attention_mask
      before SDPA -- same scatter pattern as deepseek_v4.
    - Index branch still runs its own SDPA over (idx_q, idx_k, idx_v) and
      adds the result through `o_proj`, matching sglang's parallel index
      output path. `disable_index_value` layers skip the value side.

Tiny VLM / text-only LM forward passes still work end-to-end via
AutoModelForImageTextToText / AutoModelForCausalLM.
author: Arthur Zucker

message:
KV-cache the sparse index branch + add minimax.py generation demo

Cache:
* Add MiniMaxM3VLSparseCacheLayer(DynamicLayer) — auto-registers under
  layer_type "minimax_m3_sparse". Stores idx_keys / idx_values alongside
  the main K/V so the lightning indexer can keep scoring against the full
  prefix during decode without recomputing it. Mirrors the
  DeepseekV4CSACache pattern (per-layer cache instance reached via
  past_key_values.layers[layer_idx]; custom update_index method analogous
  to DSv4's store_compression_weights / update_compressor_states).
* Register the new value in `ALLOWED_LAYER_TYPES` so the strict-dataclass
  validator accepts mixed ["full_attention", ..., "minimax_m3_sparse"]
  layer_types on the text config.
* Derive layer_types from sparse_attention_freq inside
  MiniMaxM3VLTextModel.__init__ so old checkpoints without a layer_types
  field still dispatch the right cache class.

Indexer:
* MiniMaxM3VLIndexer.forward now takes position_ids + past_key_values +
  layer_idx, looks up the per-layer cache, calls update_index with the
  new token's (idx_k, idx_v), and scores idx_q against the full cached
  idx_keys (not just the new token's idx_k). Block_bias and the index
  branch's own SDPA both use absolute query positions so causality works
  for Sq < Sk during decode.

Demo:
* minimax.py runs the image+text → AutoProcessor → .generate() round-trip
  on the tiny model. Tiny model config now uses the real 200064 vocab and
  the snapshot image_token_index (200025), so the snapshot tokenizer +
  chat_template drop in unchanged.
* Drop the no-longer-needed `image_processor_class`/`video_processor_class`
  attrs on MiniMaxM3VLProcessor (the AutoImageProcessor /
  AutoVideoProcessor registrations cover the lookup and the explicit
  strings emit a deprecation warning).

Verified:
* Cached prefill + decode argmax matches the no-cache full forward.
* model.generate() produces tokens end-to-end.
* minimax.py runs on the tiny model with the snapshot tokenizer.
author: Arthur Zucker

message:
Fix dynamic module loading from symlinked Hub cache (snapshots -> blobs)

`_compute_local_source_files_hash` resolved the module file path before
discovering its relative imports. In a standard HF/NGC hub cache, the files in
`snapshots/<rev>/` are symlinks into the content-addressed `blobs/` directory,
so `.resolve()` moved relative-import discovery into `blobs/`, where only
opaque hash-named files exist. `get_relative_import_files` then looked for
`blobs/<name>.py` siblings that do not exist, raising `FileNotFoundError` when
loading a `trust_remote_code` model with relative imports from such a cache.

Discover relative imports from the unresolved snapshot path (keeping traversal
inside `snapshots/<rev>/`, where the named `*.py` symlinks live) and only
canonicalize the parent directory when building the hash key, so a snapshot
symlink is keyed by its logical name rather than its blob target. Symlinks are
still followed transparently at `read_bytes()`, so file content is hashed
correctly. For plain (non-symlinked) local directories the produced hash is
unchanged.

Regression introduced in v5.10.1 by #46022.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

message:
Fix stale phonemizer_lang in Wav2Vec2PhonemeCTCTokenizer.phonemize (issue #46614)

phonemize() re-created the espeak backend when the requested lang differed
from self.phonemizer_lang but never updated self.phonemizer_lang. Any later
call with the original lang then compared equal, skipped re-init, and ran
the cached wrong-language backend silently.

Add self.phonemizer_lang = phonemizer_lang inside the re-init branch,
mirroring the pattern already used in prepare_for_tokenization (L226-228).
Add regression test that interleaves two languages and asserts the first
language's output is stable after a mid-stream switch.

message:
Fix dynamic RoPE not resetting inv_freq when layer_type is None

dynamic_frequency_update reads self.max_seq_len_cached but writes the
updated length to f"{layer_type}_max_seq_len_cached" (lines 110, 118).
When layer_type is None this targets a stray None_max_seq_len_cached
attribute, so self.max_seq_len_cached is never updated: the reset branch
never fires and inv_freq stays scaled after a long sequence instead of
resetting for the following short sequences.

Use prefix for both setattr calls so the write matches the read at
line 91. The layer_type is not None path is unchanged. Add a regression
test driving a long then short sequence.

message:
fix: restore RNG after mid-epoch skip, not before fetch

The previous swap only moved _load_rng_state() ahead of
get_batch_samples(). skip_first_batches still materializes discarded
batches inside the first next(epoch_iterator), advancing the global RNG
between restore and the first real training batch.

Drop skip_first_batches for mid-epoch resume. Fast-forward the epoch
iterator through the skipped micro-batches, restore the checkpoint RNG
stream, then fetch. Matches the epoch-boundary path semantics.

Add regression test. Verified with the #39215 repro.

Fixes #39215

message:
Fix silent weight re-initialization for custom PreTrainedModel subclasses

`from_pretrained` returned a randomly-initialized model (with a clean
`loading_info`: empty missing/unexpected/mismatched keys, no warning) for custom
out-of-tree `PreTrainedModel` subclasses whose `_init_weights` writes parameters
in-place (e.g. `module.weight.data.normal_(...)`).

The param-level `_is_hf_initialized` backstop in `_initialize_weights`, which
skips a module when its directly-owned params/buffers are already loaded, was
gated behind `is_remote_code`. A custom subclass loaded directly (not via
`trust_remote_code`) has `is_remote_code() == False`, so the backstop was skipped
and `_init_weights` overwrote the freshly-loaded weights.

Apply the backstop to all models, but only skip a module that actually owns
params/buffers (with all of them already initialized). A module that owns none
(its params live in child modules) may still initialize those children via parent
dispatch in `_init_weights` - e.g. a T5-style attention block initializes its
`q`/`k`/`v`/`o` projections - so it must keep running `_init_weights`. In-tree
models are otherwise unaffected: their `_init_weights` go through the guarded
`torch.init` / `transformers.initialization` helpers, which already no-op on
already-initialized tensors.

Fixes #46620
author: Shardul Deshpande

message:
Lfm2: also thread `seq_idx` through ShortConv.slow_forward (non-fast-path)

Followup to #46588. When `causal_conv1d` isn't installed (e.g. AMD ROCm
containers), `Lfm2ShortConv.forward` falls back to `slow_forward` which
uses plain `nn.Conv1d` and slides across the full packed sequence — so
the boundary-smear bug persists exactly as it did before #46588 on that
path. Add a `seq_idx` arg to `slow_forward`; when supplied (and there's
no kv cache), slice `Bx` by segment boundaries derived from `seq_idx`
and conv each packed sample independently, matching what
`causal_conv1d_fn(..., seq_idx=...)` does on the fast path.

Validated end-to-end in verl (LFM2-MoE 8B RL training, AMD MI300X,
ROCm, no causal_conv1d): step-1 k3_kl drops 0.171 -> 0.093 once both
the fast-path (#46588) and this slow-path patch are applied (the
fast-path patch alone was a no-op there).

Same change to `Lfm2MoeShortConv.slow_forward` applied manually (the
modular converter currently doesn't re-resolve a parent body whose
subclass is `class Lfm2MoeShortConv(Lfm2ShortConv): pass`, same caveat
as #46588).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

message:
Fix non-idempotent revert_weight_conversion corrupting trust_remote_code saves

When a model is saved without having been loaded through the conversion engine
(`model._weight_conversions is None`) -- e.g. a `trust_remote_code` model whose
remote `from_pretrained` builds the state dict directly, or a `from_config` model
-- `revert_weight_conversion` fabricates the mapping from the hardcoded table and
applies its reverse. For a mapping whose reverse rename is not idempotent (an
unanchored rename whose target is a substring of its source, e.g. nomic_bert and
jina_embeddings_v3 `WeightRenaming("encoder.layers", "layers")`), reverting keys
that are already in on-disk layout double-prepends the prefix
(`encoder.layers` -> `encoder.encoder.layers`), silently dropping every affected
weight on reload.

Only revert the fabricated mapping when the in-memory keys are actually in the
canonical (post-load) layout: if applying the forward renamings would still rename
a key, the keys are already in disk layout and must not be reverted. This preserves
the documented `from_config` -> original-format round-trip while fixing the
corruption for the whole substring-doubling family (nomic_bert, jina_embeddings_v3,
llava and aliases, emu3, colqwen2, detr).
author: Mingyu Sung

message:
Gate the revert skip on trust_remote_code instead of a forward-rename heuristic

The previous "would the forward mapping rename any key?" check false-positived on
native `from_config` models whose canonical keys still match an unanchored forward
rename (e.g. dinov3_vit `(?<!model.)layer.`, qianfan_ocr `encoder.layers.`), wrongly
skipping their legitimate revert and breaking `test_reverse_loading_mapping` for
~10 models.

Disk-layout in-memory keys only occur for `trust_remote_code` models (the remote
modeling code builds the state dict directly and never runs the conversion engine);
native models always carry canonical keys. Skip the fabricated revert only when the
model class lives in the dynamic `transformers_modules` namespace, leaving native
models untouched.
author: Mingyu Sung

message:
Tighten the transformers_modules namespace check and make test registration idempotent

Match `transformers_modules.` with a trailing dot so an unrelated top-level module that merely
starts with `transformers_modules` cannot match, and pass `overwrite=True` when registering the
test conversion mappings so the tests can be re-run in the same process.
author: Mingyu Sung

message:
fix: reorder cache for all cache key names during beam search

`_beam_search` hardcoded `"past_key_values"` when checking whether to
call `reorder_cache`. Models that store their cache under a different key
(e.g. Mamba uses `"cache_params"`) never had their cache reordered,
silently producing incorrect output during beam search.

Fix by iterating over `ALL_CACHE_NAMES` (the same list already used in
`_update_model_kwargs_for_generation` and the return-dict assembly block)
to find whichever cache key is actually present in `model_kwargs`.

Adds a regression test on `MambaForCausalLM` that spies on
`DynamicCache.reorder_cache` and asserts it is called once per generated
token during beam search.

Fixes #46612
author: Arnav Kewalram

message:
fix: guard reorder_cache call with hasattr to preserve RWKV/xLSTM behavior

Models whose cache doesn't implement reorder_cache (RWKV uses a plain
list, xLSTM uses xLSTMCache) would crash after the ALL_CACHE_NAMES
lookup found their state key. Adding hasattr guard restores pre-fix
behavior for those models while still calling reorder_cache for models
that implement it (e.g. Mamba's DynamicCache).

message:
fix(masking): cast tensor q_offset/kv_offset to int in flex_attention_mask

A cache may return the query/kv offsets as 0-d tensors (StaticLayer does this to
avoid graph breaks). On the eager/sdpa path they are only added to a 1-D arange and
stay harmless, but flex's mask_mod is vmapped over scalar q_idx/kv_idx, so adding a
tensor injects an extra dimension and makes the BlockMask >4D, raising a ValueError.
Cast the offsets to Python ints inside flex_attention_mask only, leaving the eager/sdpa
path untouched.

message:
fix(sdpa): add MPS workarounds for bidirectional attention and value head dim bugs

For PyTorch < 2.12:
- v_dim < q_dim: pad value to q_dim before SDPA, then slice output back to original v_dim.
- v_dim == q_dim: no-op.
- v_dim > q_dim: skip the padding workaround and warn, because negative padding would crop value and corrupt the output.

message:
Fix: honor cache_config["max_cache_len"] on the static-cache path

cache_config is documented as configuring the KV cache, but on the static
path it was read only inside the quantized branch, so an explicit
cache_config={"max_cache_len": N} was ignored and the cache was sized to
max_length-1. Thread it into _prepare_static_cache, guarded with max() so the
cache is never under-sized. No behavior change when cache_config is unset.

Adds a regression test in the no-download CacheTest class.

message:
fix: block Hub kernel repo patterns in attn_implementation from config.json

The fix for CVE-2026-4372 blocks _attn_implementation_internal from being
set via config.json. However, the public field attn_implementation is
processed by kwargs.pop() before the exclusion check and triggers a
property setter that writes directly to _attn_implementation_internal,
bypassing the fix.

This patch validates that attn_implementation values loaded from config
do not contain '/' (Hub repo pattern). Standard values (eager, sdpa,
flash_attention_2, etc.) are unaffected. Users can still set kernel
implementations explicitly via from_pretrained(attn_implementation=...).

message:
fix(aria): use math.ceil in get_number_of_image_patches to match actual patch count

`get_number_of_image_patches` used floor division (`//`) to compute the
number of patches, but `get_image_patches` calls `divide_to_patches` which
iterates `range(0, height, patch_size)` — equivalent to ceiling division.

When `max_image_size=980` the default `split_resolutions` include odd
multiples of 490 (490, 1470, 2450, ...) that are not divisible by 980.
The mismatch caused `get_number_of_image_patches` to undercount — returning
0 instead of 1 for resolutions like [490, 980] or [980, 490], and 1 instead
of 2 for [980, 1470] or [1470, 980].

The wrong count propagates to `num_image_tokens` in `get_multimodal_data`,
misaligning visual embeddings with text token positions at inference time.

Fix: replace `resized_height // max_image_size * resized_width // max_image_size`
with `math.ceil(resized_height / max_image_size) * math.ceil(resized_width / max_image_size)`.

The same change is applied to all three files (modular source + two
auto-generated variants). Also updates the existing test whose expected
value was based on the old formula, and adds regression cases for the
`max_image_size=980` scenarios that were previously returning 0.

Fixes #46728
author: Arnav Kewalram

message:
Fix Mamba2 mixer chunked-prefill/speculative decode for Zamba2 and Nemotron-H

The Mamba2 mixer took the single-step decode branch whenever the cache
held a previous state, ignoring seq_len. For seq_len > 1 with a non-empty
cache (chunked prefill, speculative/assisted decode verification) it used
only the first tokens dt plus one conv/SSM step, silently producing wrong
logits for the rest of the chunk and corrupting the cached state.

Gate the single-step branch on seq_len == 1; for the multi-token cached
case run the full path seeded from the cache (prepend the cached conv
left context, pass the cached recurrent state as the initial SSM state).

Same root cause and fix as mamba2 (#46032); applied in modular_zamba2.py
so the change propagates to nemotron_h, which inherits the mixer.

Signed-off-by: Ting Sun <suntcrick@gmail.com>
author: Ting Sun

message:
Prepend the full cached conv_states in the Mamba2 chunked path

Match mamba2 (#46084) and the qwen3-next reference by prepending the full
cached conv_states instead of conv_states[:, :, 1:]. The two are numerically
identical: the extra oldest column only adds one warm-up conv position that the
trailing [-seq_len:] slice drops. This keeps the chunked-prefill left-context
consistent with the reference path.

Signed-off-by: Ting Sun <suntcrick@gmail.com>
author: Ting Sun

message:
Fix Mamba2 cuda_kernels_forward chunked-prefill for Zamba2 and Nemotron-H

cuda_kernels_forward had the same defect as the torch path: a multi-token
forward with a primed cache (chunked prefill / speculative verification) was
routed into the single-step causal_conv1d_update / selective_state_update
kernels and crashed ("weight must have shape (dim, width)"). Gate the
single-step branch on seq_len == 1; for the multi-token cached case run the
full kernels seeded from the cache (prepend the cached conv left-context and
drop it after the conv, pass the cached recurrent state as initial_states to
mamba_chunk_scan_combined), mirroring #46084.

Signed-off-by: Ting Sun <suntcrick@gmail.com>
author: Ting Sun

message:
Align Zamba2/Nemotron-H chunked-prefill tests with merged mamba2 test

Reshape the regression test to match the form merged in #46084: a model-level
first-token causal check (the first position of a multi-token cached forward must
equal the single-token cached forward), with separate CPU and accelerator variants.
Nemotron-H has no CPU variant because its MoE layers use grouped_mm, which has no
CPU kernel.

Signed-off-by: Ting Sun <suntcrick@gmail.com>
author: Ting Sun

message:
Propagate Mamba2 chunked-prefill cache fix to Bamba, FalconH1, GraniteMoeHybrid

These models carry their own Mamba2 mixer and gate the single-step branch on
seq_len == 1, but their multi-token cached path still starts from a zero conv
context and zero SSM state, so chunked-prefill / speculative verification is
history-less. Seed the multi-token cached path from the cache (prepend the cached
conv left-context, pass the cached recurrent state as the SSM initial state),
mirroring the Zamba2/Nemotron-H fix and #46084. Add the chunked-prefill regression
test to each.

Signed-off-by: Ting Sun <suntcrick@gmail.com>
author: Ting Sun

message:
Extract cached conv/recurrent state into locals in the Mamba2 mixers

Bind the cached conv_states / recurrent_states once after use_precomputed_states, matching mamba2, instead of re-reading cache_params.layers[self.layer_idx] inline in the cat / initial_states / single-step paths. Pure refactor, no behavior change.

Signed-off-by: Ting SUN <suntcrick@gmail.com>
author: Ting SUN

message:
Add CPU variant for the nemotron_h chunked-prefill test

NemotronH MoE experts dispatch through integrations.moe to torch._grouped_mm, which only gained a CPU kernel in torch 2.11; guard the new _cpu test with require_torch_greater_or_equal("2.11") so it runs on current CI and skips on older torch.

Signed-off-by: Ting SUN <suntcrick@gmail.com>

message:
Fall back to the for-loop grouped_mm on CPU

torch._grouped_mm has no CPU kernel on supported torch versions (e.g. 2.8 raises
NotImplementedError), but _can_use_grouped_mm returned True for CPU tensors as
long as they were 16-byte aligned, so the grouped-mm experts path called
torch._grouped_mm on CPU and every MoE model using it crashed. Route CPU through
the for-loop fallback for torch <= 2.10, which the comment already described as
the intended CPU path.

Signed-off-by: Ting Sun <suntcrick@gmail.com>
author: Ting Sun

message:
Only fall back on CPU below torch 2.9; keep alignment gate for 2.9/2.10

torch 2.8 has no CPU kernel for _grouped_mm (raises NotImplementedError), while
2.9/2.10 have one that just needs 16 byte alignment. Restore the original
misalignment gate and only force the for-loop unconditionally below 2.9, so the
CPU kernel is still used where it works. Drop the dedicated test: the crash only
happens on torch <= 2.8 CPU, which CI (torch 2.11) never exercises.

Signed-off-by: Ting Sun <suntcrick@gmail.com>
author: Ting Sun

message:
List the grouped_mm CPU fall-back cases one per line

Per review: flatten the condition so each fall-back case is its own or-clause
(dynamo non-bf16; CPU <= 2.10 misaligned; CPU <= 2.8 with no kernel at all), and
list them one by one in the comment.

message:
Fix offloaded cache device mismatch on hybrid models

Generating with cache_implementation="offloaded" crashes on hybrid models
that interleave attention and linear-attention (Mamba/SSM) layers, with a
cpu/accelerator device mismatch when concatenating the KV cache.

Only attention layers go through the offloading update path; the prefetch of
the next layer kept landing on the interleaved linear-attention layers (a
no-op) and never brought the next attention layers KV back to the accelerator,
so after prefill every attention layers KV was stranded on CPU.

Make prefetch skip layers that are not offloaded and target the next attention
layer that actually goes through the offloading update path, wrapping around if
needed. This is a no-op for non-hybrid caches.

Signed-off-by: Ting Sun <suntcrick@gmail.com>

message:
Round the ue8m0 FP8 scale before quantizing so dequant matches the stored inverse

For scale_fmt="ue8m0", Fp8Quantize._quantize_one quantized the weight with the
unrounded block scale but stored a weight_scale_inv rounded up to a power of two.
Fp8Dequantize multiplies by that stored inverse, so the round-trip was off by up to
a full octave per block. Round the inverse scale first, re-derive the forward scale
from it, then quantize, matching DeepGEMM's order. The scale_fmt="float" path is
unchanged.

message:
feat(examples): add minimal Tensor Parallelism training example with Trainer (#41141)

Adds examples/pytorch/tensor_parallelism/tp_training.py, a focused,
runnable example of TP-only training using Trainer.

Key points:
- Load model with tp_plan='auto' (the only TP-specific flag needed)
- Trainer auto-detects model.tp_size and creates ParallelismConfig
- Uses SmolLM2-1.7B + TinyStories for low-resource reproducibility
- Explains common pitfalls: device_map='auto', init_empty_weights()
- Shows explicit ParallelismConfig usage as a comment for 3D parallelism

Fixes #41141

message:
Fix left-padding token selection in BioGptForSequenceClassification

BioGptForSequenceClassification was the last decoder classification head
still using the legacy pooling index (input_ids != pad).sum(-1) - 1, which
is the last real token only under right padding. With left padding the real
tokens are shifted to the end, so the head pooled an earlier token and
returned silently wrong logits and loss. Use the same left/right-padding
safe selection as the other decoders (from #35911).

Signed-off-by: Ting Sun <suntcrick@gmail.com>

Fix Moonshine training-loss double-shift

Moonshine right-shifts labels into decoder_input_ids, then computes the loss via

plain CrossEntropyLoss instead, matching Whisper/Bart and the VisionEncoderDecoder
fix in #40863. Adds a regression test (with and without -100 padding).

message:
fix(tokenization): guard against IndexError when apply_chat_template gets empty list

When `apply_chat_template` is called with an empty list `[]`, line 3089 checks:

```python
if isinstance(conversation, (list, tuple)) and (
    isinstance(conversation[0], (list, tuple)) or hasattr(conversation[0], "messages")
):
```

`conversation[0]` raises `IndexError` because the list is empty.

Fix: add `conversation and` before accessing the first element, so the short-circuit prevents the index access on empty sequences.

Reproduction:
```python
from transformers import AutoTokenizer
tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained("Qwen/Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct")
tokenizer.apply_chat_template([], tokenize=False)  # IndexError before fix
```

Fixes #46752.

message:
Fix prompt lookup decoding crash when no EOS token is configured

PromptLookupCandidateGenerator.get_candidates calls torch.isin(chosen_ids,
self.eos_token_id) once an ngram match is found, but self.eos_token_id is None
whenever no EOS token is in effect, e.g. open-ended generation with
eos_token_id=None (which is not refilled from the config) or a base checkpoint
with no eos_token_id. torch.isin(Tensor, None) then raises "TypeError: isin()
received an invalid combination of arguments". Guard the EOS-masking branch with
an is-not-None check, matching the guard already used for the same tensor
elsewhere in this file.

Signed-off-by: Ting Sun <suntcrick@gmail.com>

message:
Do not pin the gated delta net norm to cuda:0 with a hardcoded device

The gated RMSNorm inside the GDN mixer was built with
device=torch.cuda.current_device(), forcing that one norm onto cuda:0
regardless of the module target device whenever the FusedRMSNormGated
(flash-linear-attention) branch is taken. Every sibling submodule follows
the default/target device, so a plain construction strands only this norm
on cuda:0 and a later CPU or cuda:1 forward hits a device mismatch. Drop the
argument so the norm is placed by the normal loading / .to() / device_map
path like the rest of the module.

Affects qwen3_next, qwen3_5, qwen3_5_moe and olmo_hybrid; the modular
sources qwen3_next and olmo_hybrid regenerate the four modeling files.

Signed-off-by: Ting Sun <suntcrick@gmail.com>

fix: convert Tensor q_offset/kv_offset to int in flex_attention_mask

When q_offset or kv_offset are computed from tensor operations

Python ints. This adds an extra dimension to the mask, causing
create_block_mask to produce a 5D tensor instead of 4D.

Convert to Python int via .item() at the function boundary.

Fixes #46682

message:
Fix garbage generation for Qwen3.5/Qwen3-Next under device_map CPU offload

GatedDeltaNet models read self.conv1d.weight directly in the decode path to
feed causal_conv1d_update, without going through self.conv1d.forward. Under
accelerate device_map offload, a module's weights are only moved onto the
compute device by its AlignDevicesHook when its forward runs, so at decode
conv1d.weight stays on the meta device and the convolution runs on weights
with no data, producing garbage from the first decoded token, while prefill
(which calls self.conv1d) stays correct. Fire conv1d's offload hook to
materialize its weights before the convolution.

Signed-off-by: Ting Sun <suntcrick@gmail.com>

message:
fix(gemma4): disable bidirectional attention on global layers for vision tokens

For Gemma 4, vision tokens should only attend bidirectionally within local
(sliding window) layers, not global (full attention) layers.

Previously, this implementation shared a single
create_masks_for_vision_model function (defined in modular_gemma3.py)
between both Gemma 3 and Gemma 4. This was incorrect because:

- Gemma 3 uses a single unified mask with bidirectional attention baked in for ALL layers
  (including global).
- Gemma 4 explicitly disables bidirectional attention on global layers.

This commit:
- Keeps Gemma 3's create_masks_for_vision_model unchanged (OR(causal,
  blockwise) on global layers).
- Adds a Gemma 4-specific create_masks_for_vision_model in
  modular_gemma4.py that uses causal-only masking for global layers.
author: Douglas Reid

message:
fix: convert past_length to int in _prefill for static cache compatibility

StaticCache.get_seq_length() returns self.cumulative_length which is
intentionally a device tensor (for torch.compile). But _prefill uses it
in arithmetic that produces next_sequence_length, which is later passed
to prepare_inputs_for_generation(next_sequence_length: int | None).

When next_sequence_length is a tensor instead of int, the slice
input_ids[:, -next_sequence_length:] produces wrong (0-length) results,
causing model.forward to crash on view(1, 0, -1, 128).

Fix: convert past_length to Python int in _prefill before arithmetic.
This preserves cumulative_length as tensor on the model (for compile)
while ensuring correct slice behavior in the generation loop.

Fixes #46858

Fix RT-DETR extra feature level projection indexing (#46832)

When num_feature_levels exceeds backbone output count, the first extra
decoder projection must receive the last backbone feature tensor before
conv2d, not index [-1] on the conv output. Mirror the correct pattern
already used in the following loop iteration.

Also honor generation_config.cache_config["max_cache_len"] for static
caches (in addition to generation_config.max_cache_len) so documented
cache_config path sizes StaticCache correctly (#46424).
author: Aravindhan Thaninayagam

message:
Fix GatedDeltaNet torch fallback prefill/decode divergence (#46190)

The pure-torch `torch_chunk_gated_delta_rule` zero-padded incomplete final
chunks to chunk_size=64. Pad tokens still updated the recurrent state, so a
full-sequence forward of length T and a cached prefill of length T-1 produced
inconsistent states for the shared prefix (different pad counts). Single-token
decode then ran the recurrent step on that bad state, yielding up to ~0.125
logit drift vs full forward on Qwen3.5 (torch fallback / no FLA).

Route the torch chunk fallback through `torch_recurrent_gated_delta_rule` so

message:
fix(mps): remove float64 casts in MusicFlamingo and UDOP that break Apple Silicon MPS

MPS does not support torch.float64. Two functions unconditionally upcasted
to float64 before computation and then cast back, which raises a TypeError
on MPS devices.

- `apply_rotary_time_emb` in MusicFlamingo: removed the float64 upcast
  entirely; the result was cast back to the original dtype anyway, so the
  upcast added no precision benefit. Also regenerated modeling_musicflamingo.py
  from the modular file.
- `combine_image_text_embeddings` in UDOP: used `.float()` (float32) instead
  of `.to(torch.float64)` for the bbox mean comparisons against 0.0 and 1.0,
  where float32 precision is fully sufficient.

Fixes #46723

message:
Fix Florence2 training-loss double-shift (same pattern as Moonshine #46784)

Florence2ForConditionalGeneration shifts labels right via shift_tokens_right()
to create decoder_input_ids, then calls self.loss_function() which maps to
ForCausalLMLoss — that function shifts labels again internally. This causes
the model to train against labels[..., 1:] instead of labels.

Replace self.loss_function() with plain CrossEntropyLoss, matching the fix
applied to Moonshine in #46784 and CohereASR.

message:
Reject assisted generation for LFM2 and LFM2-MoE (set _is_stateful)

LFM2 and LFM2-MoE are conv/attention hybrids that keep recurrent conv state,
but inherited the default _is_stateful = False from Llama. Assisted and
prompt-lookup decoding were therefore not rejected, and since their conv state
cannot be rolled back during speculative verification they silently produced
wrong tokens (divergent from greedy) instead of raising the clear "assisted
generation is not supported with stateful models" error the other stateful
models raise. Set _is_stateful = True on both.

message:
Fix silent SDPA math-kernel fallback for GQA with head_dim > 256

GQA layers with head_dim > 256 (e.g. Gemma4's head_dim=512 full-attention layers) passed enable_gqa=True to SDPA whenever attention_mask is None. PyTorch's mem-efficient and flash SDPA kernels do not support the enable_gqa broadcast at head_dim > 256, so SDPA silently fell back to the math kernel, materializing the S x S scores (O(S^2) memory) and OOMing at long sequences.

Make use_gqa_in_sdpa return False for head_dim > 256 so we take the repeat_kv (MHA) path, which the mem-efficient kernel supports at head_dim=512. Behavior is unchanged for head_dim <= 256.

Signed-off-by: Butterfingrz <2395959141@qq.com>
author: Butterfingrz

message:
Fix silent SDPA math-kernel fallback for GQA when key/value head_dim differ

At differing key/value head_dim (e.g. MLA models) SDPA's Flash kernel is
disqualified (it requires query/key/value to share the last dim) and the
mem-efficient kernel does not support the enable_gqa broadcast, so
enable_gqa=True is served only by cuDNN and silently falls back to the math
kernel (O(S^2)) on builds/GPUs without the cuDNN SDPA backend.

Require key head_dim == value head_dim (<= 256) in use_gqa_in_sdpa so
GQA-in-SDPA is only used when the Flash kernel can portably serve it;
otherwise take the repeat_kv (MHA) path, which the mem-efficient kernel
supports at differing head_dim. Add a regression test.

Signed-off-by: Butterfingrz <2395959141@qq.com>
author: Butterfingrz

message:
fix(masking): preserve dict return type in create_masks_for_generate single-pattern path

PR #41251 introduced an early-return path in create_masks_for_generate that
returns a bare mask value (instead of a dict) when effective_config.layer_types
contains a single unique pattern. Downstream forward methods like
Qwen3Model.forward use isinstance(attention_mask, dict) to detect whether the
mask was already prepared by generate(), so a bare return causes the model to
silently rebuild the mask, defeating the purpose of precomputation.

Wrap the single-pattern early-return in {layer_pattern: mask} to keep the
dict contract. The unknown-types path (L1561) is left untouched: returning the
raw attention_mask there is a deliberate 'defer to model' fallback whose
semantics differ from the dict path.

Fixes #46962

author: fyhon

message:
Add QNN (Qualcomm HTP) backend to the ExecuTorch exporter

Shift-left of QNN export from optimum-executorch into transformers: exporting an HF
model with ExecutorchConfig(backend="qnn") now produces a QNN-delegated .pte directly.

New src/transformers/exporters/qnn_utils.py with three hooks, wired into the exporter's
backend dispatch:
- prepare_for_qnn: pre-export prep — static-KV-cache wrap for decoder-only LMs (decode-capable
  graph; avoids the constant-folded-mask "No graph inputs present" QNN serialization failure) +
  HTP compiler spec (fp16, or int for the quantized path).
- quantize_for_qnn (_POST_EXPORT_TRANSFORM): PT2E 8a8w/16a4w quantization AFTER torch.export,
  which is where QNN quantizes (QnnQuantizer annotates the exported graph, then convert_pt2e).
- _lower_for_qnn (_BACKEND_LOWER): backend-specific lowering that runs QNN's pre-export + edge
  pass pipeline inside a QnnManagerContext. The exporter's generic to_edge_transform_and_lower
  can't lower QNN (hits KeyError aten.alias_copy.default / rank-3 FullyConnected / "No QnnManager
  active") because those passes never run.

Exporter plumbing:
- Add a _POST_EXPORT_TRANSFORM hook (runs on the ExportedProgram between export and lowering)
  and a _BACKEND_LOWER table (backend supplies its own lowering; default stays the generic path).
- ExecutorchConfig gains soc_model / quantize / static_cache / max_cache_len / calibration_inputs
  (all QNN-only; ignored by xnnpack/cuda).
- integrations/executorch.py: TorchExportableModuleForDecoderOnlyLM.forward now threads
  cache_position into the static-cache module (it was dropped, breaking the decode graph).

Validated on Qwen2.5-0.5B -> fully QnnBackend-delegated .pte (FP16, SM8750 and SM8550).

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

message:
MLA: fuse low-rank up-projection into Q for absorbed attention (eager/sdpa)

Avoid materializing the decompressed K/V during eager/SDPA forward by
absorbing the kv_b_proj weight (W_uk, W_uv) into the attention computation:
- Q-nope @ W_uk gives absorbed queries in compressed space
- Softmax scores @ compressed_k @ W_uv^T gives the value output
- Full K/V expansion is deferred to KV-cache update only

Applied to DeepSeek-V2, V3, and V3.2 (DSA) MLA attention.
Falls back to standard expansion for flash-attention backends and
decode steps with a populated cache.

message:
Fix MoonshineStreaming right-window off-by-one in sliding_window_mask_function

sliding_window_mask_function used a strict less-than for the right context:
right_mask = (dist < 0) & (-dist < right_window_size). With right_window_size=4
that lets a query attend to only 3 future frames, but the Moonshine v2 paper
(arXiv:2602.12241) defines w_right=4 as a 4x20ms = 80ms lookahead (4 future
frames). Use <= so right_window_size future frames are attended.

Applied to both the modular source and the generated modeling file.

message:
Replace mecab-python3 with fugashi

This replaces mecab-python3 with fugashi for Japanese tokenization. I am
the maintainer of both projects.

Both projects are MeCab wrappers, so the underlying C++ code is the
same. fugashi is the newer wrapper and doesn't use SWIG, so for basic
use of the MeCab API it's easier to use.

This code insures the use of a version of ipadic installed via pip,
which should make versioning and tracking down issues easier.

fugashi has wheels for Windows, OSX, and Linux, which will help with
issues with installing old versions of mecab-python3 on Windows.
Compared to mecab-python3, because fugashi doesn't use SWIG, it doesn't
require a C++ runtime to be installed on Windows.

In adding this change I removed some code dealing with `cursor`,
`token_start`, and `token_end` variables. These variables didn't seem to
be used for anything, it is unclear to me why they were there.

I ran the tests and they passed, though I couldn't figure out how to run
the slow tests (`--runslow` gave an error) and didn't try testing with
Tensorflow.

message:
Replace flake8-rst with flake8-rst-docstrings

Fixes #46865

Replace `flake8-rst` with `flake8-rst-docstrings` for running flake8 on code snippets in rst files.

* **pyproject.toml**
  - Remove `flake8` from the `tool.ruff.lint` section.
  - Add `flake8-rst-docstrings` to the `tool.ruff.lint` section.
  - Update the `tool.ruff.lint` section to use `flake8-rst-docstrings` instead of `flake8-rst`.

* **.pre-commit-config.yaml**
  - Remove the `flake8-rst` hook from the `hooks` section.
  - Add a new hook for `flake8-rst-docstrings` in the `hooks` section.
  - Configure the new `flake8-rst-docstrings` hook with appropriate `additional_dependencies`.

---

For more details, open the [Copilot Workspace session](https://copilot-workspace.githubnext.com/pandas-dev/pandas/issues/46865?shareId=XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX).

message:
BUG: Categorical.map() sort categories for unordered categoricals (GH#58153)

Categorical.map() preserved the positional order of categories from the
original (pre-mapped) categorical. For unordered categoricals, this meant
the mapped values inherited an arbitrary category ordering, causing
sort_values(key=...) to ignore custom sort orders.

Fix: In Categorical.map(), when the categorical is unordered, sort the
mapped categories and remap codes accordingly. For ordered categoricals,
preserve the existing category order (since the ordering is user-defined).

Mixed-type categories (e.g. str and float) that cannot be compared
gracefully fall back to preserving the original category order.

Closes #58153

Generated-by: GitHub Copilot
author: Tine Zivic

message:
BUG: Fix DataFrame.var/std/sem(axis=1) returning object dtype with mixed numeric types (GH#55194)

When a DataFrame has columns of mixed numeric dtypes (e.g. int and float),
var/std/sem(axis=1) was dispatched through _reduce_axis1 which called
nanops functions that preserve the object dtype of the internal block
rather than upcasting to float64.

Fix: explicitly include var, std, and sem in _reduce_axis1, mirroring
the pattern already used for mean/median. This ensures the result is
always float64 when all input dtypes are numeric.

Added edge case tests for NaN-containing rows, all-NaN rows, and
ddof exceeding valid count.
author: Tine Zivic

message:
BUG: Fix AttributeError for str[i] on ArrowExtensionArray (GH#65112)

ArrowExtensionArray (used when dtype_backend='pyarrow' with convert_dtypes)
inherited ArrowStringArrayMixin but was missing _str_getitem, which is
needed by the str accessor's __getitem__ method.

Add _str_getitem to ArrowStringArrayMixin delegating to _str_get (for
integer keys) and _str_slice (for slice keys), matching the
ObjectStringArrayMixin implementation.

Closes #65112
author: Tine Zivic

message:
BUG: raise on melt output-name collisions for var_name (GH#65654)

`DataFrame.melt` already rejects `value_name` values that collide with
an existing column. There was no analogous check for `var_name`, so when
`var_name` resolved to a label that was also an `id_var`, the
`value_name`, or a duplicate within a list-like `var_name`, the result
was a frame with duplicate output columns and silently overwritten
`id_vars` data.

Repro:

    >>> df = pd.DataFrame({"id": [1, 2], "a": [10, 20], "b": [100, 200]})
    >>> df.melt(id_vars="id", var_name="id")
      id id  value
    0  a  a     10
    1  a  a     20
    2  b  b    100
    3  b  b    200

The `id` column's original values (1, 2) are gone.

After this patch the same call raises:

    ValueError: var_name produces duplicate output column names with
    id_vars / value_name; duplicates: ['id']

The check covers all three collision shapes (id_vars, value_name, and
intra-list duplicates) and runs after `var_name` is normalized to a
list, so it works for both scalar and MultiIndex inputs.

Tests added in `test_melt.py` for each shape.
author: Sanjay

message:
DOC: fix incorrect alignment description in where/mask docstrings

The Notes section for Series.where, DataFrame.where, Series.mask, and
DataFrame.mask incorrectly stated that alignment is done between `other`
and `cond`. The actual behavior is that `self` is aligned against `cond`,
not `other`. Misaligned positions between `self` and `cond` are filled
with False (where) or True (mask).

Closes #61781
author: RonGamzu

message:
BUG: strftime %Y not zero-padded for years < 1000

The directive-map fast path added in GH#64609 mapped "%Y" to "{0}", dropping the zero-padding that datetime.strftime / Timestamp.strftime apply, so Series.dt.strftime and DatetimeIndex.strftime rendered years < 1000 unpadded (e.g. "5" instead of "0005"). Map "%Y" to "{0:04d}" to restore 4-digit-minimum padding. See GH#58179.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
author: Brock

message:
BUG: get_indexer_non_unique raising ZeroDivisionError on empty index

The binary-search heuristic divided by 2 * n.bit_length(), which is zero
for an empty index. An empty index matches nothing, so return an
all-missing result early. Affects both the masked and non-masked engines.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
author: Brock

message:
BUG: unstack corrupting source DataFrame via Copy-on-Write aliasing

The zero-copy fast path added in GH-65107 makes _Unstacker.get_new_values
return a view of the block values when the MultiIndex is already sorted.
For multi-block (non-consolidated) frames, Block._unstack built the result
block with refs=None, so Copy-on-Write did not track the sharing and
mutating the unstack result wrote through to the source DataFrame.

Track the reference when the unstacked values alias self.values, mirroring
the add_references logic in _Unstacker.get_result, so the zero-copy view is
preserved and CoW triggers a copy before either object is mutated.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
author: Brock

message:
BUG: DataFrame(list_of_arrays) with ragged same-dtype rows

The uniform-dtype fast path added in GH-64958 derived the column count
from the first row only (ncols = len(data[0])) and indexed every row by
that width. A longer first row raised IndexError; a shorter first row
silently dropped trailing values. Gate the fast path on uniform row
lengths so ragged rows fall through to the object/pad path and are
padded to max width with NaN, matching list-of-lists behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
author: Brock

message:
TST: pin int64 in ragged-array constructor tests for 32-bit platforms

The new ragged tests compared a result built from default-dtype numpy
arrays (platform int, i.e. int32 on 32-bit) against an expected built
from Python int lists (always int64), which mismatched on Linux-32-bit
and Pyodide. Pin the input arrays to int64 so the result dtype is
int64 on every platform.

BUG: inplace where/mask corrupting multi-column 2D datetimelike blocks

EABackedBlock.putmask reoriented mask to the block's storage layout but
not new, so an inplace where/mask on a multi-column 2D extension block
(datetime64/timedelta64/datetime64tz/period) filled masked cells from the
wrong column. Transpose new alongside mask, mirroring the value = value.T
that EABackedBlock.setitem already applies. Regression from GH#64620,
unreleased, so no whatsnew note.

message:
PERF: restore to_datetime caching for duplicated input with an explicit format

GH-65409 added an O(1) early-bail to ``should_cache`` that disabled
caching whenever an explicit ``format`` was passed, on the assumption
that ``array_strptime`` is always fast enough to make caching unhelpful.
That only holds for ISO-style formats. For slower strptime formats
(e.g. ``%d%b%y``, ``%z`` offsets, ``%d-%m-%Y``) on highly-duplicated
input, caching parses only the unique values and is a large win, so the
bail caused 5-15x slowdowns (reported by the asv perf tracker:
pandas-dev/asv-runner#137 -- ToDatetimeFormat.time_exact,
time_same_offset, time_different_offset_to_utc, and
ParseDateComparison.time_to_datetime_format_DD_MM_YYYY).

Drop the ``format`` bail (and the now-unused ``format`` parameter on
``should_cache``); format inputs again go through the normal
duplication-based cache decision. The genuinely-safe O(1) bails for
``unit`` and already-datetime dtypes are kept.

Also move the GH-65409 whatsnew note out of the frozen v3.0.0 file (that
PR is not in any released 3.0.x) and into v3.1.0, scoped to the cases
that actually improve.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

message:
BUG: HDFStore.select bus error on strict-alignment platforms (GH#54396)

PyTables stores compound types packed, so a float64 values block that
follows an odd-width field (e.g. a string Index) is byte-unaligned.
DataCol.convert extracted the field as a view, so the unaligned buffer
flowed into the result block and SIGBUS'd in take on 32-bit ARM. Copy to
realign when the extracted field is not aligned.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

message:
BUG: HDFStore.remove with large "in" where clause deleted all rows (GH#17567)

A where clause selecting on more than 31 values (e.g. "index in [...]") is
realized as a post-read filter rather than a numexpr condition. Selection.
select_coords ignored that filter and returned every row coordinate, so
HDFStore.remove deleted the entire table instead of only the matching rows.

Apply the filter inside select_coords so both read_coordinates and delete
get the correct coordinates from a single code path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

message:
BUG: read_hdf categorical column with category equal to nan_rep (GH#21741)

A category string equal to the nan_rep (default "nan") is decoded back to
a NaN category on read. Stripping that category subtracted a per-category
cumsum from per-row codes, which raised a broadcasting ValueError whenever
#rows != #categories (and silently mis-mapped codes otherwise). Build an
explicit old->new code remap instead, mapping codes that pointed at a
dropped NaN category to -1.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

message:
BUG: HDFStore.select raises clear error for columns-in-where with iterator/chunksize (GH#12953)

A column selection in the `where` expression (e.g. "columns=['A']") is a
projection rather than a row filter, so it cannot be applied via the row
coordinates used for chunked reads. Previously this combination raised a
cryptic `KeyError: 'column [columns] not found in the table'`. `HDFStore.select`
now raises an informative `NotImplementedError` pointing to the equivalent
`columns` argument, which already works with iteration.

BUG: to_hdf fixed format ignored compression for object columns (GH#45286)

GenericFixed.write_array stored object-dtype (e.g. string) columns in a
PyTables VLArray without passing the store's filters, so complib/complevel
were silently ignored for these columns. String-heavy frames written with
format="fixed" were therefore left uncompressed, producing far larger files
than expected. Pass filters=self._filters on this path, matching the sibling
BaseStringArray and carray paths.

message:
BUG: read_hdf fails on old files with freq=None on non-datetimelike indexes (GH#33186)

Old pandas (<=0.15.x) wrote a freq=None attr on every index node, including
non-datetimelike ones. The read path treated the mere presence of the freq
attr as a signal to route a plain Index through the TimedeltaIndex factory,
raising TypeError/ValueError. Only treat a non-None freq as meaningful.

WARN: read_hdf nested OR query may hit PyTables index bug (GH#50598)

results due to an upstream PyTables bug in its index-accelerated query
path. Detect that exact query shape and warn, pointing at the

message:
BUG: HDFStore.select where query on categorical column matched NaN rows

A `where` query on a categorical data column for a value that is not one
of the categories was converted to the code -1, which is also the sentinel
pandas uses for missing (NaN) values. The query therefore matched NaN rows
the user never requested (and, symmetrically, dropped them from != queries).
Map non-category values to a sentinel distinct from -1 so such queries match
no rows for ==/in and all rows for !=, consistent with in-memory Categorical
comparison semantics.

GH#22977

BUG: fixed-format HDFStore storer shape reported a phantom row for empty objects

get_storer(...).shape returned (1, N) for a fixed-format Series or

transposed layout used for non-empty blocks).

message:
BUG: Apply into parameter to nested mappings in DataFrame.to_dict(orient='index')

Fixes pandas-dev/pandas#65778

When using DataFrame.to_dict(orient='index', into=...), the into parameter
was only applied to the outer mapping. Inner row mappings were always
plain dict, ignoring the into argument.

This commit replaces all three dict() calls in the orient='index' branch
with into_c(), ensuring both outer and nested mappings use the specified
mapping constructor.
author: godfengliang

message:
BUG: fix assert_series_equal check_series_type not respected for ndarray subclasses

Closes #65770

- Added check_class parameter to assert_numpy_array_equal (default True)
- When check_class=False, ndarray subclasses are treated as equivalent
- assert_series_equal now passes check_series_type to assert_numpy_array_equal
  when the exact comparison path is taken
- Added tests for both assert_numpy_array_equal and assert_series_equal
author: godfengliang

message:
BUG: fix check_exact not passed through to assert_extension_array_equal

In assert_series_equal, when check_exact=True and the Series is backed by
ExtensionArrays, the check_exact, rtol, and atol parameters were not forwarded
to assert_extension_array_equal. This caused check_exact=True to be silently
ignored for float ExtensionArrays, which would fall back to approximate
comparison with default tolerances.

- Pass check_exact, rtol, atol through to assert_extension_array_equal
- Add regression test with Float64 dtype
author: godfengliang

message:
BUG: fix assert_frame_equal by_blocks path dropping check_exact/rtol/atol/check_categorical

The by_blocks=True path in assert_frame_equal was not forwarding
check_exact, rtol, atol, and check_categorical parameters to the
recursive assert_frame_equal calls. This caused check_exact=True to
be silently ignored when by_blocks=True, falling back to approximate
comparison with default tolerances.
author: godfengliang

message:
BUG: fix assert_index_equal dropping params in CategoricalIndex recursion

The recursive assert_index_equal call for CategoricalIndex.categories
was passing only the 'exact' parameter, silently dropping check_names,
check_exact, check_categorical, rtol, and atol. This meant the categories
comparison always used default values regardless of caller overrides.

Fix by forwarding all relevant parameters, matching the pattern already
used in the MultiIndex recursion path.
author: godfengliang

message:
BUG: fix assert_interval_array_equal exact param not forwarded

The 'exact' parameter was accepted and documented but never passed
through to the internal assert_equal calls for left._left and left._right.
This meant any caller setting exact=False or exact='equiv' had their
value silently ignored.
author: godfengliang

message:
BUG: apply `into` to nested mappings in `to_dict(orient='index')`

Fixes GH#65778

When `DataFrame.to_dict(orient='index', into=...)` was called, the
outer mapping respected the requested `into` type, but each inner
per-row mapping was constructed with the built-in `dict()` /
dict-comprehension, ignoring `into` entirely.

For example, with a custom mapping `class MyDict(dict): pass`:

    df = DataFrame({'A': [1, 2], 'B': ['x', 'y']})
    out = df.to_dict(orient='index', into=MyDict)
    type(out)              # MyDict  (correct)
    type(out[0])           # dict    (wrong, should be MyDict)

This was inconsistent with `orient='records'`, which already applied
`into` to each per-row mapping.

The fix wraps the inner mapping construction in `into_c(...)` in all
three sub-branches of the `orient == 'index'` branch (all-object
columns, mixed/extension columns, all-non-object columns), mirroring the
existing `orient == 'records'` pattern.

message:
BUG: escape SQL identifiers in ADBCDatabase to prevent injection (GH#65065)

Add _get_valid_adbc_name() helper function that applies ANSI SQL
double-quote escaping to table and schema names, mirroring the
existing _get_valid_sqlite_name() pattern.

Apply quoting to the three vulnerable call sites in ADBCDatabase:
- read_table: SELECT ... FROM {table}
- to_sql: DROP TABLE {table} (if_exists='replace')
- delete_rows: DELETE FROM {table}

Note: the adbc_ingest() call in to_sql is unaffected as it accepts
name and schema as separate parameters handled by the ADBC driver.

Add regression test test_adbc_identifier_quoting covering all three
code paths with a table name containing a space.

message:
CI: pin pytest to <9.1 in pip-based unit-test jobs

pytest 9.1.0 (released 2026-06-13) made duplicate parametrization of an already-parametrized fixture an error rather than a warning, breaking collection of 4 tests on 2.3.x (test_new_axis, test_intersection, test_replace_series_datetime_tz, test_to_html). This surfaces on the Linux-32-bit job because it installs pytest via pip without a cap; the conda-based jobs are still on pytest 9.0.x. Cap to <9.1 in the three pip install lines, as in GH#65883 on main.
author: Gabriel Hackebeil

message:
CI: cap matplotlib to <3.11 in CI/dev environments

matplotlib 3.11.0 (released a few days ago) deprecated the locs attribute and changed color-comparison semantics, causing ~200 plotting tests to fail across the unit-test matrix (test_time_formatter in test_converter.py, test_default_colors_named_undefined_prop_cycle in test_style.py, test_df_legend_labels_time_series in test_frame_legend.py, etc.). main has GH#65742 (only test_style/test_series/test_boxplot fixes) but that doesn't cover test_converter or test_frame_legend on 2.3.x. Cap matplotlib in the conda env yamls, environment.yml, and requirements-dev.txt to restore green CI; pandas 2.3.x does not target matplotlib 3.11. Leave pyproject.toml's install extras unbounded (matches main; the validate-min-versions-in-sync hook only tolerates a bare '>=' there).
author: Gabriel Hackebeil

message:
CI: cap pytest to <9.1 in conda env yamls and dev requirements

Conda-forge now serves pytest 9.1.0, so the conda-based unit-test jobs (e.g. Copy-on-Write 3.10) hit the same 4 duplicate-parametrization collection errors that previously only affected the pip-based Linux-32-bit job. Extend the cap from the earlier pip-only fix to the conda env yamls, environment.yml, and requirements-dev.txt. The min-versions yaml is left at pytest>=7.3.2 because validate_min_versions_in_sync.py scans for the literal '- pytest>=' substring there; if the Min Versions job hits 9.1.0 we will address it separately. pyproject.toml is left as-is for the same hook reason.

message:
CI: pin windows runner to windows-2022 for Python 3.9 SSL compat

GitHub flipped windows-latest to windows-2025, whose trust store contains a cert with malformed ASN.1 data that Python 3.9's ssl.create_default_context() can't handle, raising ssl.SSLError: [ASN1: NOT_ENOUGH_DATA] at aiohttp's module load (triggered transitively by td.skip_if_installed('gcsfs') in pandas/tests/io/test_gcs.py). Python 3.10+ silently skips bad certs; 3.9 doesn't and won't get a backport. Main no longer supports 3.9 so there is no upstream fix to mirror. Pin the Windows runner in the unit-test matrix to windows-2022, which has an older trust store that doesn't trigger this. windows-2022 is still a supported image and is appropriate for the 2.3.x maintenance branch.
author: Gabriel Hackebeil

message:
TST: skip test_gcs.py on Windows + Python 3.9 to avoid SSL cert error

Pinning windows-latest to windows-2022 did not fix the SSL collection error reported earlier: windows-2022 has the same offending cert in its trust store as windows-2025, so the import chain td.skip_if_installed('gcsfs') -> gcsfs -> aiohttp -> ssl.create_default_context still raises ssl.SSLError: [ASN1: NOT_ENOUGH_DATA] during pytest collection on Python 3.9 (3.10+ silently skips bad certs). Since the failure fires at module load, the only way to avoid it is to prevent collection of the file in this configuration. Add a module-level pytest.skip guarded by sys.platform == 'win32' and Python < 3.10. Also revert the windows-2022 pin to windows-latest now that it serves no purpose.

message:
BUG: Fix DataFrame.any/all(skipna=False) crash with pd.NA (#65710)

When calling `DataFrame.any(skipna=False)` or `DataFrame.all(skipna=False)`
with `BooleanArray` containing `pd.NA`, pandas previously crashed with a
`ValueError`. This was because `BaseMaskedArray._wrap_na_result` defaulted
to an `Int64` datatype for the `pd.NA` scalar for these logical reductions
instead of `boolean`. The mismatched type then caused a failed downcast
to numpy `bool`.

This commit explicitly assigns `"bool"` dtype for `any` and `all`
reductions in `_wrap_na_result` and adds a regression test.

message:
BUG: assert_series_equal(check_dtype=False) raises on masked pd.NA vs object pd.NA (#61473)

In the check_exact path a masked array (e.g. Int32) holding pd.NA was
converted with to_numpy(), which coerces pd.NA to nan and returns a float
array. That nan no longer matched the pd.NA stored in the object array on
the other side, so the comparison failed under check_dtype=False even
though the values were equal. Convert with to_numpy(dtype=object) so pd.NA
is preserved on both sides.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
author: Rufus Gordon-Heywood

message:
TST: wrap parametrize iterables in list() for pytest 9.1+

pytest 9.1.0 deprecates passing non-Collection iterables (generators,
zip, chain, permutations, combinations, product) as parametrize
argvalues (pytest-dev/pytest#13409). pandas' filterwarnings promotes the
resulting PytestRemovedIn10Warning to an error, which broke test
collection. Wrap the affected argvalues in list() so collection works on
pytest 9.1+ and ahead of the hard error coming in pytest 10. No behavior
change: the materialized values are identical.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

message:
BUG: improve error message for non-string dtype with DB-API connection (GH#61385)

When using a raw DB-API connection (e.g. sqlite3 without SQLAlchemy),
to_sql(dtype=...) requires SQL type strings. Passing a SQLAlchemy
type object previously raised an unhelpful error showing only the
column name and repr of the type.

This adds a clearer message explaining the constraint and suggesting
the use of a SQLAlchemy engine for SQLAlchemy types, per maintainer
guidance on the issue.

No fallback conversion is added, consistent with the maintainer's
stated preference that pandas not own such conversion logic.

message:
PERF: vectorize is_range_indexer and is_sequence_range scans

4x-unroll the inner scan loops of is_range_indexer and is_sequence_range
in lib.pyx, combining lanes with bitwise | (not `or`) so the compiler can
emit vectorized comparisons, with a scalar tail for the remainder. Mirrors
the existing has_nans/all_nans unrolling (GH-65192).

On the common full-scan path (array is a range / sequence) this is up to
~1.9x faster for is_range_indexer and ~1.7x for is_sequence_range at 100k+
elements, with no regression on the early-exit short-circuit path.

Split out from GH-65298.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

message:
PERF: add lib.has_sentinel for integer-indexer sentinel scans

Add a fused-type (int8/16/32/64) Cython helper has_sentinel(arr, sentinel),
a short-circuiting, allocation-free equivalent of (arr == sentinel).any()
with an 8x-unrolled inner loop, and wire up the clear (indexer == -1).any()
call sites in merge (non-inner), single-level unstack, groupby key lifting,
MultiIndex NaN-key indexing, and DataFrame.__setitem__ with non-unique
columns.

Split out from GH#65298. The unrolling of the pre-existing has_infs /
is_range_indexer / is_sequence_range helpers is a separate, independent PR.

TYP/TST: matplotlib 3.11 typing compat and 32-bit test_datetimes fix

Preliminary fixes toward getting the scheduled dependency-bump PR
(GH-65918) green:

- matplotlib 3.11 narrowed the accepted key type of rc_context and the
  return type of Axes.pie, producing mypy errors under the bump. Widen
  the affected locals to Any so type-checking passes on both 3.10 and
  3.11 (warn_unused_ignores rules out a version-specific # type: ignore).
- pandas/tests/arrays/test_datetimes.py used np.arange(10) (int32 on
  32-bit platforms) before .view("timedelta64[ns]"), reinterpreting the
  buffer into out-of-range values that overflow on multiply and fail
  collection on Linux-32-bit. Use an explicit i8 base, matching the line
  above. This also fixes a check currently failing on main.

message:
BUG: TextReader() with no arguments segfaults (GH#53131)

When pandas._libs.parsers.TextReader was instantiated with no source
argument, __cinit__ raised a TypeError before parser_new() ran, leaving
the cdef parser pointer NULL. The ensuing __dealloc__ called
parser_cleanup on the NULL pointer, dereferencing member fields and
segfaulting.

Guard parser_cleanup against a NULL parser so the TypeError propagates
cleanly instead of crashing the interpreter.

message:
BUG: Timedelta ISO parsing rejected >2-digit hour/minute components (GH#48122)

The hand-written ISO 8601 duration parser capped hour and minute
components at two digits, so e.g. ``Timedelta("PT100M")`` raised
``ValueError`` while ``Timedelta("PT99M")`` parsed fine. The cap was
inconsistent with the seconds/weeks/days components (which have no
such limit) and with ISO 8601, which places no digit-count restriction
on duration components. Remove the check.

message:
BUG: refs not set in DataFrame ctor from Series/Index with dtype="str" (GH#63936)

With infer_string=False, dtype="str" resolves to numpy '<U', which
ndarray_to_mgr realizes via ensure_string_array(copy=False) -- a view of
the source when it already holds strings. astype_is_view(object, '<U') is
False, so refs were dropped even though memory was shared, breaking CoW
tracking (mutating the DataFrame corrupted the source).

Attach the source references when the realized block actually shares
memory with the source, checked after sanitize_array.

message:
BUG: fix DataFrame.duplicated() when no columns or subset=[]

Fixes two related bugs in DataFrame.duplicated() (GH#61191):

1. A DataFrame with rows but zero columns returned an empty Series
   (wrong values, no index) because the early-exit guard used
   `self.empty`, which is True whenever any axis has length 0 —
   including the column axis. Changed guard to `len(self) == 0`
   so only truly row-less DataFrames short-circuit.

2. Calling duplicated(subset=[]) raised:
     ValueError: not enough values to unpack (expected 2, got 0)
   because zip(*map(f, vals), strict=True) on an empty iterator
   could not unpack into (labels, shape). Added an explicit branch
   before the zip path: when subset is empty every row is
   identical, so we mark all rows after the first as duplicates.

Tests added in pandas/tests/frame/methods/test_duplicated_no_columns.py:
- TestDuplicatedNoColumns (31 tests) — int/str/float/MultiIndex,
  all three keep= modes, single-row and empty DataFrame edge cases
- TestDuplicatedEmptySubset — subset=[] with all three keep= modes
- TestDuplicatedRegressions — confirms all pre-existing behaviour
  is unchanged

message:
CI: Avoid CPython 3.11.0 segfault in Minimum Versions build

The minimum-versions pixi environment uses a lowest-direct solve, which
pinned Python to exactly 3.11.0. Under PYTHONDEVMODE, pytest formats the
traceback of unraisable exceptions, and 3.11.0 segfaults doing so
(python/cpython#99729, fixed in 3.11.1), crashing test workers
intermittently. Floor the environment to python >=3.11.1.

message:
CI: Disable pytest cacheprovider in pyodide wheel test

The pyodide cibuildwheel test runs pd.test() over the installed package,
and pytest's cacheprovider writes a pytest-cache-files-* dir into
site-packages/pandas/ with permissions the host runner can't traverse
under emscripten. cibuildwheel's post-test shutil.rmtree then fails with
PermissionError, failing the wheel build even though tests pass. Disable
the cache for that run so the dir is never created.

logic re-read the terminal width via shutil.get_terminal_size() and ignored
an explicitly set display.width. The width option is now honored: the
display.width default becomes None (auto-detect when interactive, no
truncation in non-interactive sessions), and both the string- and
format-level fit paths use the resolved width instead of the raw terminal
size.

message:
BUG: read_csv c engine loses float precision with thousands separator (GH#44145)

precise_xstrtod only routed to the correctly-rounded fast_float parser when no
thousands separator was set, so read_csv(thousands=...) fell back to the bespoke
parser and could be off by up to a couple of ULP on long-mantissa floats. Strip
the integer-part separators into a scratch buffer and hand the token to
fast_float, keeping the strict acceptance of the fallback for malformed input.

message:
BUG: DataFrame.combine_first loses precision for wide integers (GH#60128)

instead of reindexing self to the row union, which promoted integer columns
through float64 and lost precision for values outside its exactly-representable
range. Fully-covered columns now keep their dtype and exact values, including
with duplicate row or column labels.

message:
BUG: fix segfault adding non-tick DateOffset to datetime data under threads

The vectorized offset apply path (shift_months/shift_quarters ->
_roll_qtrday/get_day_of_month) compared the Python ``str`` day_opt inside
``with nogil`` blocks, i.e. with the GIL released. Concurrent Python
activity in another thread (e.g. pytest-xdist's execnet receiver) could
race those comparisons and segfault.

Convert day_opt to a C enum before releasing the GIL so the in-nogil
comparisons are pure C. This backports the memory-safety core of the
day_opt-enum refactor in GH-64480 without the rest of that PR's changes.

message:
CLN: remove genuinely-unreachable dead code (GH#27396)

Triaging mypy's --warn-unreachable surfaced a handful of branches that
can never execute at runtime. Remove them:

- groupby/categorical.py: drop the second `if sort` block, which is
  preceded by an `if sort: return c` so it can never run.
- groupby pct_change: fill_method is validated to be None just above,
  so the if/else collapses to ffill.
- masked: result is always a plain ndarray here (the tuple case returns
  earlier), so the isinstance(result, TimedeltaArray) guard never holds.
- internals/blocks interpolate: the `method == "asfreq"` branch is
  pre-empted by get_interp_index, which raises for that method first.
- SingleBlockManager._equal_values: `other` is typed Self, so the
  `other.ndim != 1` guard is always False.
- internals/construction: ensure_index converts list-of-lists columns
  to a MultiIndex upstream, so _validate_or_indexify_columns never sees
  a raw list and the is_mi_list block is dead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
author: Brock

message:
BUG: fix NULL dereference and use-after-free in ujson C encoder

Closes #65903
Closes #65904
Closes #65905

Three related memory-safety bugs in the vendored ujson C encoder
and datetime C extension:

1. date_conversions.c int64ToIso (GH#65903)
   When make_iso_8601_datetime fails, result is freed via
   PyObject_Free but execution continues to strlen(result),
   causing use-after-free (undefined behaviour / potential crash).
   Fix: add 'return NULL' immediately after PyObject_Free.

2. objToJSON.c get_long_attr (GH#65904)
   PyObject_GetAttrString can return NULL on error. The result
   was passed directly to PyLong_Check / PyLong_AsLongLong
   without a NULL guard, causing a potential NULL dereference.
   Fix: early-return NPY_MIN_INT64 when value is NULL.

3. objToJSON.c total_seconds (GH#65905)
   PyObject_CallMethod can return NULL when total_seconds() raises.
   The result was passed directly to PyFloat_AS_DOUBLE (an unsafe
   macro with no type/NULL check) and then Py_DECREF, both of
   which crash on NULL.  Additionally, PyFloat_AS_DOUBLE bypasses
   type checking even for non-NULL values.
   Fix: NULL guard returning -1.0, switch to safe PyFloat_AsDouble.
author: Naman Agrawal

message:
REF: decouple get_dummies column selection from select_dtypes

get_dummies determined which columns to encode via
select_dtypes(include=["object", "string", "category"]), which relied on
select_dtypes' internal ArrowDtype .numpy_dtype unwrap to also pick up
Arrow-backed string/dictionary columns (GH#56273). Express the intended
column set directly with a local _is_encodable predicate fed to
BlockManager._get_data_subset instead, so get_dummies no longer depends
on select_dtypes' string-alias matching of EA/Arrow columns. Behavior is
unchanged.

message:
DOC: fix broken "See Also" cross-reference links in API docs (GH#31661)

numpydoc emits "See Also" entries such as ``DataFrame.pipe`` as
non-module-qualified ``:py:obj:`` references. For methods documented under a
non-``pandas`` module (e.g. ``pandas.api.typing.DataFrameGroupBy``), Sphinx
prepends the current module and never falls back to the top-level ``pandas``
namespace, so these links rendered as plain text instead of hyperlinks.

Add a ``missing-reference`` handler that retries unresolved ``py`` references
with a ``pandas.`` prefix, restoring the links across GroupBy, Resampler, and
rolling/expanding/ewm docs without any docstring changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

message:
BUG: select_dtypes ExtensionDtype matching (GH#40234, GH#59888)

select_dtypes reduced every ExtensionDtype spec to the numpy scalar type it
shares (via infer_dtype_from_object), so "Int64"/Int64Dtype also matched numpy
int64 (GH#40234), and passing a parameterized EA class (ArrowDtype,
DatetimeTZDtype) emitted a spurious "Instantiating X without any arguments"
UserWarning and matched the wrong columns.

Match EA specs by identity instead: an instance by equality, and a class -- or
a bare class-name string like "Int64"/"string"/"category" -- as a kind that
matches any instance of that class, with a small Arrow bridge so StringDtype /
CategoricalDtype still match Arrow string/dictionary columns. The numpy path is
untouched: numpy types (np.int64), kind strings ("int64", "datetime"), and
bracketed strings ("datetime64[ns]") behave exactly as before; that
cross-backend kind matching and the numpy-type tightening remain in GH#65366.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

message:
REF: select_dtypes match ExtensionDtype specs by identity only

Drop the Arrow kind-bridge so an ExtensionDtype spec (instance, class, or bare
class-name string) selects columns of that dtype-class by isinstance/equality
and nothing cross-backend: pd.StringDtype matches StringDtype columns,
pd.CategoricalDtype matches Categorical columns. Cross-backend kind matching
(e.g. "string"/"category" also selecting Arrow string/dictionary columns) is
left to the kind-string bucket in GH#65366.

message:
BUG: select_dtypes nullable dtype matching numpy columns (GH#40234)

A nullable masked request ("Int64", Int64Dtype, or Int64Dtype()) selected
numpy columns sharing its scalar type (e.g. int64) in addition to the masked
columns, and passing the class emitted a spurious UserWarning. Route masked
dtype specs to class-identity matching so they select only their own columns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
author: Brock

message:
BUG: select_dtypes ExtensionDtype class spec matching (GH#59888)

Stacked on the GH#40234 masked-dtype fix. Passing an ExtensionDtype subclass
(e.g. pd.ArrowDtype, pd.DatetimeTZDtype) to select_dtypes selected the wrong
columns and emitted a spurious "Instantiating X without any arguments"
UserWarning, because the class was routed through pandas_dtype(cls). Route an
EA class spec to isinstance ("any column of this class") matching instead, so
such a class selects every column of that type without warning
(StringDtype/CategoricalDtype also match the Arrow string/dictionary kinds).
EA instances and kind-string cross-backend matching remain in GH#65366.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
author: Brock

message:
BUG: select_dtypes ExtensionDtype instance and bracketed-string matching (GH#59888)

A non-masked ExtensionDtype instance or a bracketed extension string now
selects only columns of exactly that dtype, rather than collapsing to the
scalar type it shares with columns of other backends. Fixes the Arrow string
forms "timestamp[ns][pyarrow]" (matched the wrong columns) and "int64[pyarrow]"
(matched none).

Split off from GH-65366; independent of GH-66096 and GH-66097.

message:
BUG: Fix length_of_indexer for negative-step slices (GH#66100)

The manual slice-length computation in length_of_indexer incorrectly
calculated the length for negative-step slices (e.g. [::-2] on a 5-element
array returned -2 instead of 3), causing iloc __setitem__ to raise a false
ValueError when the value length actually matched the slice.

Replace the error-prone manual formula with len(range(*indexer.indices(n))),
which delegates to CPython's built-in slice.indices() and handles all edge
cases (None start/stop, negative step, out-of-bounds) correctly.

Closes #66100
